data.title: Migrate 4 consumers to PRECONDITIONS, add compaction on epic-closure with ordering contract, and legacy-ticket graceful-degrade
data.description:
## Scope
Migrate four existing consumers (harvest-worktree, completion-verifier, merge-to-main archive, ticket CLI list/show) to read PRECONDITIONS events. Add compaction-on-closure with explicit ordering contract relative to Story 4's epic-closure validator. Define legacy-ticket graceful degradation.

## Done Definitions
- **harvest-worktree** reads PRECONDITIONS via `_read_latest_preconditions` and consumes latest per-stage summary; no behavioral regression on existing harvests.
- **completion-verifier** reads upstream PRECONDITIONS for its story/epic closure checks (coordinated with Story 4's epic-closure validator write path via the ordering contract below).
- **merge-to-main archive** reads PRECONDITIONS to populate archive summary; mixed-format archives (pre-rollout + rollout tickets) render correctly.
- **ticket CLI list/show** reads PRECONDITIONS to display manifest summary; legacy tickets (no PRECONDITIONS events) render with "pre-manifest" placeholder; no blocking, no synthesis.
- **Compaction on epic closure**: after epic-closure validator exits, rewrite all PRECONDITIONS events for the epic into one snapshot event `<ts>-<uuid>-PRECONDITIONS-SNAPSHOT.json`; individual events marked retired.
- **Ordering contract pinned**: (1) epic-closure validator runs BEFORE compaction; (2) compaction uses atomic rename to publish snapshot; (3) Story 1's `_read_latest_preconditions` transparently handles both flat events and snapshot format (snapshot returned only when no live events remain, or snapshot timestamp ≥ all live events); (4) readers retry-once on transient file-missing during compaction window.
- **Legacy-ticket handling**: all four migrated consumers gracefully degrade when a ticket has zero PRECONDITIONS events — log once per (consumer, ticket), proceed without blocking, never synthesize fake events. Mixed-corpus tests (pre-rollout + rollout tickets) pass for every consumer.
- Per-consumer unit tests + integration tests covering the pre-rollout/rollout boundary.
- p95 latency budget per consumer migration step (target TBD in Story 7 contract; measured in Story 6 benchmark).

## Considerations
- [Reliability] Compaction races with concurrent reads — the atomic-rename + retry-once contract must be tested with a fault-injection harness.
- [Maintainability] Legacy-ticket degradation is operational-readiness — document the diagnostic log format so operators can distinguish "missing event" from "corrupt event".
- [Consumer impact] Completion-verifier is both a Story 4 writer and a Story 5 reader — Story 4 depends on this story to avoid the double-modification race.
